text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that allows developers to work on different tasks without interfering with the main codebase. When starting a new feature, developers create a feature branch from the main branch. This creates a separate workspace where changes can be made safely. After completing the feature, the changes are integrated back through merging. Typically, this involves opening a pull request, which lets team members review the code before it is combined. This workflow helps maintain a clean, stable main branch while enabling parallel development. Understanding branching is essential for efficient collaboration in modern software teams.
